session_content: Cognitive Evolution of Human Preference: Intersubjectivity in Metaverse
Geographical Hypothesis by Jared Diamond (Pulitzer Prize Winner): The Logic of Agri-Tech and Monsanto
Institutions on Innovation & Industrial Revolution by Daron Acemoglu (Nobel Nominee): Why do startups incorporate in US/Singapore?
Market for Ideas by Joel Mokyr (Nobel Nominee): Will the blockchain really empower creators?
Technological Progress in Long-Run by Robert Solow (Nobel Laureate) Why do governments subsidize innovations?
Technological Progress in Short-Run by Edward Prescott (Nobel Laureate) Elon Musk’s First Principles and Euler Theorem
Innovation on Inequality by Angus Deaton (Nobel Laureate) AI & Robotics with the Elasticity of Substitution
Transaction Cost Economics by Ronald Coase (Nobel Laureates) Digital Transformation as Transactions
Theory of Firm by Oliver Williamson (Nobel Laureate) Outsourcing cloud to Amazon Web Service
Incomplete Contracts by Oliver Hart (Nobel Laureate) Why do TESLA pursue vertical integration?
Theory of Non-cooperative Games by John Nash (Nobel Laureate) Oligopolistic Competition by Grab vs. Lineman
Sustainable Finance by Matthew Dearth (Nanyang Technological University)
Theory of Games with Incomplete Information by John Harsanyi (Nobel Laureate) Algorithms of Deep Minds & Chat GPT
Presentations by Team & The Quiz
Presentations by Team

session_content: AI in Context - Introduction
course_learning_objectives:
By the end of this course, students should be able to do the following:
14.2 Learning Contents
Class Day and Time: Section 1: Mondays 1 PM-4PM, Section 2: Fridays 1PM - 4PM, unless otherwise indicated below.
14.3 Method
 Lecture and discussion
 Brainstorming and discussion of case study to students
 Project based assignment
14.4 Media
 PowerPoint media delivered by the course management system
 Electronics and website media
14.5 Assignment through Network System
14.5.1 Assigning and Submitting Method: course management system
14.5.2 Learning Management System: course management system
14.6 Evaluation
Mid-Term Examination (40%), Final Examination (40%), Attendance (10%), Assignments (10%).
Attendance is compulsory and attendance information is collected at the beginning of each class. By the university regulations, 80% or more attendance is required to pass the course.
Being late, leaving in the middle of the class or disturbing the class by chatting, using one's phone is not allowed. For absences, a valid, documented reason is required.
Attendance in exams is required and passing both the midterm exam and the final exam is required to pass the course.
14.7 Class Rules and Etiquette
Students must not arrive later than 10 minutes after the class started. Students who fail to arrive in time or leave without permission will not receive attendance points for the lecture. Being late (30 min or more) means a deduction of character points.
Assignments must be submitted in time. No assignment points for late submissions.
14.8 Rules and Regulations for AI Usage
When you use AI in information gathering, ensure information usage is from credible and reliable sources.
AI tools can be used for grammar checking, proofreading and editing drafts, but review suggestions critically and make your own final edits.
For outline structures for papers and projects, ensure the final works reflect your thought, Al should be used just for the starting point.
In using AI for assignment completion, only submit the work that represents your own understanding and effort. If you submit assignment answers generated by an AI, the instructor will grade them using an AI.
AI tools cannot be used in the exam, unless explicitly allowed by the instructor, it should be your own work.
Avoid copying AI generated content directly into your assignment without proper attribution.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Fundamentals in Climate change and its connection to health. Understand the changing environmental conditions that are directly affecting human health with rising temperatures, sea-level and the impact of extreme weather events. Students will also learn about food security, water scarcity/resources and the impact of changes of biodiversity and ecosystems on human health. The students will learn the interconnection between climate change and human health in 4 main areas 1) Heat-related conditions 2) Air quality & diseases 3) Vector/water-borne diseases, zoonosis and risk of outbreaks/pandemics 4) Nutrition & agriculture (food and water security)

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Health related issues with climate change. Deep understanding of the impact of climate change on human health and the pathophysiology associated to it. 1) Heat-related conditions & cardiovascular diseases 2) Air quality, pollution & respiratory diseases 3) Vector- and water- borne diseases, zoonosis, infectious diseases and risk of outbreaks/pandemics.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Food/Nutrition security & Water scarcity. Learn about the risks associated with climate change on agriculture, food and water security. Impact on nutrition. Interconnection with human health and potential diseases associated to these changes of natural resources.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Climate change challenges in Thailand and in ASEAN*. Students will present examples of climate change events in the region that are directly impacting human health. (background, event, consequences, impact on health). The results of the research done will be prepared in small groups. Brainstorming, Q&A, discussions.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Principles of Resilience for human health. Learn about the definition of resilience linked to the impact of climate change on human health. Students will learn the importance of health risk management, public health implementation and public health policies for climate change. Will also learn the consequences of resiliency on the society and on the economy.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Thailand as a case study. Students will learn why Thailand is one of the most affected country in the world and how the country is developing as Asia’s leader in risk management on health impact from climate change. They will also gain knowledge on how big cities like Bangkok can be dramatically affected by climate change with a special emphasis on the 2011 Thailand floods – Background, flooding, damages, consequences on human health, mitigation & efforts, the future risks.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Students’ literature research on Resilience in ASEAN*. Students will present the results of literature research done on resilience programs and initiatives linked to climate change and human health in small groups. They will focus on several countries in the regions by showcasing specific topics in the region. Brainstorming, Q&A, discussions.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Innovations for climate change-related health issues 1- Heat related issues/conditions. Students will learn what is the current status of healthcare, biomedical innovations and other technologies in industry that are addressing the problem (tackling sweating, heating body, rash, sunburn, stroke and mitigating risk of cardiovascular events).

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Innovations for climate change-related health issues 2- Air quality and pollution. Students will learn what is the current status of healthcare, biomedical innovations and other technologies in industry that are addressing the problem (tackling pollution, allergy, rash, respiratory diseases, cancer, nasal spray, mask, treatments, urban heating, urban re-organization, pollution measures, etc…). Ex: Students will focus on Bangkok, its air quality and the impact on the health of Bangkokians.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Innovations for climate change-related health issues 3- Vector & water borne diseases and zoonosis. Students will learn what is the current status of healthcare, biomedical innovations and other technologies in industry that are addressing the problem and tackling the risks of outbreaks of infectious diseases, possible zoonotic diseases and impact on fragile group of populations. Ex: Students will learn about the risks in Thailand/ASEAN with climate change (Dengue, Cholera with flood, outbreaks, etc..)

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Innovations to tackle Food security and Water scarcity. Students will learn what is the current status of development of sustainable agricultural practices, water management, recombinant plants crops and seeds, crop yields to address problem of food and security (agriculture shift for the future, rice resistant to salt for floods, etc….).

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Students’ literature research on Resilience on Bangkok as big city Students will present the results of literature research done on the impact of climate change in the 3 health related issues within Bangkok mentioned above in small groups. Analysis of case-study regarding resilience plan for certain human health conditions linked to climate change and identification of particular solutions big cities.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Thailand resilience. Students will learn the actual current plan that is developed to address major challenges to the health of the Thai population and will also understand what is being done as well to tackle food and water security in the country. Students will also learn about local initiatives and innovations that are already taking place to tackle those issues.

filename: 5604202 Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change.docx
course_title: Adaptation of Health Solutions in Response to Climate Change
session_content: Course summary & critical thinking*. Discussions on ethical considerations, societal implications from the new technologies and innovations that are used to provide better health and better access to food and water (such as GMO-seeds and crops). What are the consequences of innovations on the society, on the population habits, the environment, on the biodiversity and eco-systems..?. Discussion on the considerations to prevent/mitigate vector- & water-borne diseases and risk of infectious diseases & outbreaks/pandemics.

session_content: Consumer Healthcare. Definition and principles. Historical context and evolution of consumer healthcare. The impact of consumer behavior on the healthcare industry. Why patient is owning back its own health and why this at the centre of the healthcare revolution. What does it mean for the future of medicine ? How medicine and healthcare industry are becoming consumer centric ?.

Key trends and development. Understand most important trends and key areas in this very fast pace health ecosystem. Definition of Digital Health, personalized medicine, Mobile health and new technologies into prevention space that will be shared and discussed along the development data-driven strategies and Evidence based medicine.

Customer-centric innovations. Learn about the latest innovation principles and excellence that are driving the consumer healthcare industry. Why the new model focusing on fast pace solutions towards prevention is challenging old and slow pace pharmaceutical business models.? Integration of consumer needs at the core of early stage innovation.

Case Studies of Successful Consumer-Centric Models. Analyzing companies/settings that excel in consumer healthcare. Lessons learned from successful implementations. Key takeaways for innovation and strategy development. How to Develop a Customer-Centric Strategy ? Frameworks for creating a customer-centric healthcare strategy, tools for assessing consumer needs and preferences, strategies for continuous improvement in customer engagement

Consumer Behavior & Engagement Strategies. Strategies for enhancing patient engagement. The importance of patient education and health literacy. Building trust & loyalty through communication, transparency and science. The Role of Consumer Data Analytics in Healthcare. Understanding big data and its implications. How data informs and influence customer-centric approaches.

The 5 pillars of consumer healthcare. OTC, personal care, dietary supplementation, digital health and health and wellness/wellbeing. Current trends and innovations in each pillar of CH space. Student will learn key examples of successes and failures in self-care and preventative health in Consumer healthcare.

The World of digital health, AI and big data driving the business and the competition. The future of CH and medicine? Student will learn about the Emerging technologies and the potential impact of AI, IoT on consumer healthcare, diagnostic and future medicine such as personalized medicine.

Student Presentation and Research exercise on Consumer healthcare innovation. Student will develop a presentation on customer-centric healthcare innovation, discussing issues and potential success with the implications of consumerization in healthcare.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Aging healthily. Thailand has joined developed countries as an aged population country and is expected to reach the super aged society status in the next decade. What does it mean for the country and how to manage this fast-aging process? How can new technologies and biotechnologies help tackle this issue and allow the population to age healthily?

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Communicable Diseases 1: latent & persistent infections. Learn about some latent infections that are indirect public health issues as they have been shown to highly increase cancer risks (examples of EBV, HBV, HPV). Chronic viral infections that are reservoirs for potential future outbreaks (example HIV). Should we treat symptoms, should we treat infections? How to prevent and/or cure such infections?

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Communicable Diseases 2: emerging infectious diseases. Understand pandemics and outbreaks. Analyse the consequences, both medical and economic, of outbreaks/pandemics. Case studies using examples such as COVID-19, Dengue, Ebola. What should be the public health response? How to be prepared? How can new technologies help?

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Non-Communicable Diseases 1: chronic diseases causes and consequences. Using three examples, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, high blood pressure, learn about current NCDs epidemics, their possible causes and their consequences on quality of life, on comorbidities, on country economics (reduced efficacy of manpower, higher social welfare costs).

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Non-Communicable Diseases 2: chronic diseases preventions and treatments. Discuss the ways to better prevent chronic diseases, especially with modern technologies (health status tracking, risk predictions, AI health analysis…). Understand the lifestyle factors in preventing and possibly treating some chronic diseases. Analyse the current treatments, their limitations and suggest novel therapies using innovative technologies. This module will be a mix of lecture and students’ research projects presentation.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Non-Communicable Diseases 3: cancer. What is cancer, how does it grow? What are the causes of cancer (genetic predispositions, environmental triggers, chemicals, radiations…)? The current prevalence of cancer in the world, in ASEAN and in Thailand. How to detect cancer better and earlier? New solutions for prediction and detection. Limitations, regulatory considerations.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Non-Communicable Diseases 4: cancer treatments. What is cancer, how does it grow? What are the causes of cancer (genetic predispositions, environmental triggers, chemicals, radiations…)? The current prevalence of cancer in the world, in ASEAN and in Thailand. How to detect cancer better and earlier? New solutions for prediction and detection. Limitations, regulatory considerations.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Access to medical services 1. Modern technologies in the medical field often mean expensive, bulky, fragile equipment and/or highly skilled staff, which reduces the easy spreading of such technologies. In this context, how to bring better medicine to everyone? In countries like Thailand with few big highly urbanised areas and more remote, dense-less regions, not everyone is equal towards medical services access. In this context, how can new technologies fill the gaps? How to implement telehealth, how to democratise teleconsultation? And how to make teleconsultation really effective? How to get all patients’ data to the physician in real time accurately? How to conduct some medical tests in areas with lack of equipment and skilled medical staff? How to get people living in remote areas acquainted to modern digital health technologies?

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Digital Health solutions. Can AI be a first line worker in medicine? What are the current digital health devices, what do they bring and what are their limitations? Presentations by students of wearable health tech and monitoring technologies for consumers (ECG, blood pressure, glycemia, falls…).

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Physical disabilities and infirmities. Can we fully repair body disabilities or infirmities? What can bionics and cybernetics bring today? Replace limb with robotic limbs or regrow some tissues and organs. Walk again after spine injury.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Stress and mental health. Is stress on the raise today? How does this affect mental health and how can new development in digital techs and neurosciences provide some medical/wellness relief? How to diagnose stress with biomarkers or body parameters measurement? Science and marketing, the importance to get the right validated information.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Neurodegenerative diseases. Brief presentation of Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, and their current treatments in Thailand. How can new digital and bio technologies improve early detection of these diseases and provide better treatment?

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Future Health Challenges. What can we foresee for the 2 or 3 next decades? Are there new trends regarding health and wellness? Will cancer be cured in 20-30 years? Would damaged organs/tissues be easily replaced in the future? Would new technologies reach the human dream of immortality? Can neuroscience download your mental persona in silico? Are immortal beings seen in SF movies close to our reach thanks to technology? Is it ethically or progress or not? Open discussion on what digital and bio technologies can do and how to use such tools. Are freedom and equality of human beings threatened by new health technologies? Or would these technologies free us more and erase our physical inequalities?

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Proposing Innovative Solutions 1. Students will be asked to choose one topic within the current health challenges and work in small groups in class and outside to develop a short proposal to address the challenge in an innovative way.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions.docx
course_title: Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions
session_content: Proposing Innovative Solutions 2. Groups will present their brief proposals and class will analyse the relevance, the feasibility and the possible acceptance of the proposal by the general population and by regulators.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions-S1,AY2025.docx
session_content: Course intro Aging healthily. Thailand has joined developed countries as an aged population country and is expected to reach the super aged society status in the next decade. What does it mean for the country and how to manage this fast-aging process? How can new technologies and biotechnologies help tackle this issue and allow the population to age healthily?
session_learning_objectives: Understand the biological pathways behind aging. Understand the impacts of aging populations on the society and the economy. Use multidisciplinary knowledge to imagine solutions to accompany society transition.
course_learning_objectives:
By the end of this course, students should be able to:
Identify and analyse current global health challenges.
Explore novel solutions in healthcare and their limitations.
Collaboratively develop proposals for innovative health solutions.
Evaluate the role of policy and ethics in addressing health issues.
Explore the future challenges and possible approaches to address them.
Learning Contents/ Learning Schedule
Classroom and Time: Chamchuri 10, room 310, Monday 09:00-12:00
14.3 Learning Outcomes

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions-S1,AY2025.docx
session_content: Communicable Diseases 1: latent & persistent infections. Learn about some latent infections that are indirect public health issues as they have been shown to highly increase cancer risks (examples of EBV, HBV, HPV). Chronic viral infections that are reservoirs for potential future outbreaks (example HIV). Should we treat symptoms, should we treat infections? How to prevent and/or cure such infections?
session_learning_objectives: Understand what communicable diseases are. Understand how they affect individuals and society. Using examples and multidisciplinary knowledge, be able to propose solutions and/or plans to manage epidemics and pandemics.
course_learning_objectives:
By the end of this course, students should be able to:
Identify and analyse current global health challenges.
Explore novel solutions in healthcare and their limitations.
Collaboratively develop proposals for innovative health solutions.
Evaluate the role of policy and ethics in addressing health issues.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions-S1,AY2025.docx
session_content: Communicable Diseases 2: emerging infectious diseases. Understand pandemics and outbreaks. Analyse the consequences, both medical and economic, of outbreaks/pandemics. Case studies using examples such as COVID-19, Dengue, Ebola. What should be the public health response? How to be prepared? How can new technologies help?
session_learning_objectives: Understand what communicable diseases are. Understand how they affect individuals and society. Using examples and multidisciplinary knowledge, be able to propose solutions and/or plans to manage epidemics and pandemics.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions-S1,AY2025.docx
session_content: Non-Communicable Diseases 1: chronic diseases causes and consequences. Using three examples, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, high blood pressure, learn about current NCDs epidemics, their possible causes and their consequences on quality of life, on comorbidities, on country economics (reduced efficacy of manpower, higher social welfare costs).
session_learning_objectives: Understand chronic diseases. Know the current challenging chronic diseases worldwide and understand some of their causes. Learn what could help with reducing the prevalence of such diseases.

filename: 5604311 Current Health Challenges and Innovative Solutions-S1,AY2025.docx
session_content: Future Health Challenges. What can we foresee for the 2 or 3 next decades? Are there new trends regarding health and wellness? Will cancer be cured in 20-30 years? Would damaged organs/tissues be easily replaced in the future? Would new technologies reach the human dream of immortality? Can neuroscience download your mental persona in silico? Are immortal beings seen in SF movies close to our reach thanks to technology? Is it ethically or progress or not? Open discussion on what digital and bio technologies can do and how to use such tools. Are freedom and equality of human beings threatened by new health technologies? filename: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution.docx
course_title: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution
session_content: Consumer Healthcare. Definition and principles. Historical context and evolution of consumer healthcare. The impact of consumer behavior on the healthcare industry. Why patient is owning back its own health and why this at the centre of the healthcare revolution. What does it mean for the future of medicine ? How medicine and healthcare industry are becoming consumer centric ?.

filename: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution.docx
course_title: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution
session_content: Key trends and development. Understand most important trends and key areas in this very fast pace health ecosystem. Definition of Digital Health, personalized medicine, Mobile health and new technologies into prevention space that will be shared and discussed along the development data-driven strategies and Evidence based medicine.

filename: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution.docx
course_title: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution
session_content: Customer-centric innovations. Learn about the latest innovation principles and excellence that are driving the consumer healthcare industry. Why the new model focusing on fast pace solutions towards prevention is challenging old and slow pace pharmaceutical business models.? Integration of consumer needs at the core of early stage innovation.

filename: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution.docx
course_title: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution
session_content: Case Studies of Successful Consumer-Centric Models. Analyzing companies/settings that excel in consumer healthcare. Lessons learned from successful implementations. Key takeaways for innovation and strategy development. How to Develop a Customer-Centric Strategy ? Frameworks for creating a customer-centric healthcare strategy, tools for assessing consumer needs and preferences, strategies for continuous improvement in customer engagement

filename: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution.docx
course_title: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution
session_content: Consumer Behavior & Engagement Strategies. Strategies for enhancing patient engagement. The importance of patient education and health literacy. Building trust & loyalty through communication, transparency and science. The Role of Consumer Data Analytics in Healthcare. Understanding big data and its implications. How data informs and influence customer-centric approaches.

filename: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution.docx
course_title: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution
session_content: The 5 pillars of consumer healthcare. OTC, personal care, dietary supplementation, digital health and health and wellness/wellbeing. Current trends and innovations in each pillar of CH space. Student will learn key examples of successes and failures in self-care and preventative health in Consumer healthcare.

course_title: Health and Well being_The Consumer Healthcare revolution
session_content: The World of digital health, AI and big data driving the business and the competition. The future of CH and medicine? Student will learn about the Emerging technologies and the potential impact of AI, IoT on consumer healthcare, diagnostic and future medicine such as personalized medicine.

session_content: Lecture and Discussion
course_learning_objectives:
1. Develop design concepts that respond to real-world spatial, programmatic, and branding challenges.
2. Translate ideas into clear 2D documentation (plans, sections, elevations) and expressive 3D representations (digital and physical models).
3. Explore sustainable approaches to a small-scale project including material selection, circular design, embodied energy, and accessibility
4. Strengthen communication through pin-ups, peer critiques, and formal presentations.
5. Build a foundation for entrepreneurial thinking by designing spaces that support business initiatives and customer experiences.
Learning Contents/ Learning Schedule
Classroom and Time: Chamchuri 10, Room 302 (9.00-12.00)

session_content: No class, National Holiday
Begin Again: Course Introduction
session_learning_objectives: - Understand the course structure, expectations, and key themes. - Engage in ice-breaking activities to build a collaborative learning environment.
course_learning_objectives:
By the end of this course, students will be able to:
Analyze Taylor Swift's music, lyrics, and public persona as tools for fostering awareness around contemporary social issues such as gender equality, women’s empowerment, and LGBTQ+ rights.
Evaluate the impact of Taylor Swift's legal battles (e.g., intellectual property rights, sexual harassment lawsuits) on public discourse surrounding law, advocacy, and women’s rights.
Examine the intersection of pop culture, celebrity influence, and activism in driving social and political movements in the 21st century.
Engage in discourse on the role of social media in amplifying Taylor Swift’s influence and advocacy efforts to participate in activism.
Create a reflective or analytical project (e.g., presentations, essays, or multimedia work) demonstrating the connection between art, activism, and social change.
Explore broader cultural movements like the #MeToo movement, LGBTQ+ activism, and gender equality campaigns, situating Taylor Swift’s contributions within these larger frameworks.

session_content: “Childless Cat Lady” — The Politics of Taylor
session_learning_objectives: - Analyze Taylor Swift’s decision to publicly voice her political views, endorse candidates, and encourage voter registration as a form of activism. -Examine the impact of celebrity influence on political engagement, voter turnout, and public discourse.
course_learning_objectives:
By the end of this course, students will be able to:
Analyze Taylor Swift's music, lyrics, and public persona as tools for fostering awareness around contemporary social issues such as gender equality, women’s empowerment, and LGBTQ+ rights.
Evaluate the impact of Taylor Swift's legal battles (e.g., intellectual property rights, sexual harassment lawsuits) on public discourse surrounding law, advocacy, and women’s rights.
Examine the intersection of pop culture, celebrity influence, and activism in driving social and political movements in the 21st century.
Engage in discourse on the role of social media in amplifying Taylor Swift’s influence and advocacy efforts to participate in activism.
Create a reflective or analytical project (e.g., presentations, essays, or multimedia work) demonstrating the connection between art, activism, and social change.
Explore broader cultural movements like the #MeToo movement, LGBTQ+ activism, and gender equality campaigns, situating Taylor Swift’s contributions within these larger frameworks.
Learning Contents/ Learning Schedule

Method
14.4 Media
